articles.title: Ontario Liberal leadership candidate vows to make health care more ‘competitive’
articles.description: Eric Lombardi, who launched his leadership bid last week, has been receiving support from a range of political voices, including many who say they've never voted Liberal

articles.title: Familiar Touch review – Kathleen Chalfant is wonderful in subtle, sensual memory loss drama
articles.description: Director Sarah Friedland brings impressive attention to detail to an audacious debut feature about a woman moved into a retirement community Profoundly tender and yet untainted by the slightest trace of sentiment, this intimate and frankly sensual drama follows elderly Ruth (American stage icon Kathleen Chalfant) as she adjusts to a major change in circumstances. Told with an audacious economy that unveils key details only when absolutely necessary, the film hints at what’s going on when Ruth treats the washing up rack like a toast caddy. Minutes later, a middle-aged man named Steve (H Jon Benjamin), with whom Ruth slyly flirts at first until he reveals he’s already married, arrives at her home to take her to her new home in a retirement community. When the staff there refer to Steve as Ruth’s son, the reveal is as shocking to her as it is to us. Continue reading...

articles.title: If Your Nails Keep Breaking, This $13 ‘Hard As Hoof’ Cream Might Be The Fix
articles.description: With nearly 80,000 reviews, this cream has become a favorite for improving overall nail health.

articles.title: Sleep researcher shares 4 unusual tips for falling asleep quickly — and they include watching re-runs of your fave TV show
articles.description: Tired of meditation and chamomile tea? An insomnia researcher shares the unexpected evening habits that actually help you drift off faster.

articles.title: Endocrinologist reveals the surprising foods wrecking your vaginal health — and the daily staple she still recommends
articles.description: What you eat affects everything—even down there. New York Post Wellness Editor Carly Stern sits down with Dr. Jennifer Blakemore to separate fact from fiction when it comes to vaginal health. Discover the real science behind cranberry juice, the everyday sugars that can affect your pH, and the specific diet changes that actually make a...

articles.title: What COVID is teaching doctors about the relationship between viruses and cancer
articles.description: The pandemic has left some evidence that viral infection may play a role in reawakening dormant cancer cells already present in a patient’s body before infection.

articles.title: Manitoba will have electronic patient records that can be shared between hospitals by next spring
articles.description: Every Manitoba hospital will soon begin building electronic records that will mean a patient's care history follows them from one hospital to another.

articles.title: His colon cancer was originally misdiagnosed. Now he's encouraging screening as Ontario boosts capacity
articles.description: Starting next month, Ontarians who are 45 years old will be eligible for colorectal cancer screening. Some patients and advocates question whether the province is ready for the additional pressure on the system, but Ontario says it's boosting capacity.

articles.title: Surgeon warns of 'biggest mistake' if you feel your knee 'pop'
New GLP-1 diabetes pill delivers major weight loss and blood sugar control
articles.description: A new oral GLP-1 medication helped people with type 2 diabetes dramatically improve blood sugar control and lose weight in a major clinical trial. The results suggest that highly effective diabetes treatments may soon become available in a much more convenient pill form.

articles.title: ‘I worry deeply about bioterrorism’: Is Europe sufficiently prepared for another pandemic?
articles.description: Internationally renowned for their life-saving Covid vaccine, what is Moderna doing now? Their CEO joins us in the studio to discuss bioterrorism, pandemic preparedness and cancer treatment.

articles.title: Research links low-energy falls to triage undertreatment in female brain injuries
articles.description: Female patients with traumatic brain injury (TBI) are 26% less likely to be admitted to a specialized trauma center than males, according to a study on data from Ontario published in CMAJ (Canadian Medical Association Journal) https://www.cmaj.ca/lookup/doi/10.1503/cmaj.251721. This difference persisted even after the researchers accounted for factors such as age, severity of injury, other health conditions, and socioeconomic circumstances.

articles.title: Constant digital tracking of young adults backfires, fueling parental anxiety
articles.description: Built-in smartphone apps and location sharing features allow parents to see where their children are at any moment: Did they arrive safely? Are they where they said they'd be? How far away are they if there's an emergency?

articles.title: Australia’s diphtheria outbreak shows why boosters, antibiotics, and better housing all matter
articles.description: A 131-case diphtheria outbreak in Australia’s Northern Territory showed how toxigenic Corynebacterium diphtheriae can re-emerge even in highly vaccinated populations. Genomic and epidemiological evidence linked most cases to a dominant ST381 strain, with transmission shaped by overcrowding, poor skin health, and persistent health inequities.

articles.title: Alzheimer's symptoms reverse after US woman takes mushrooms. Here's what it tells us.
articles.description: Magic mushrooms are better known for producing hallucinations and altering people’s sense of reality than for treating brain diseases. Most people ...

articles.title: Ireland’s hybrid fitness movement: ‘I just thought, I’m going to be bigger than everyone else’
articles.description: Popularity of combined strength and cardio training is boosted by group fitness races and events

articles.title: Researchers map rare DHDDS disease mechanism using lab-grown mini brains
articles.description: Variants in the DHDDS gene cause a severe neurodegenerative condition, characterized by tremors, seizures, coordination and learning difficulties, usually manifesting in early childhood. This Parkinson's-like condition is extremely rare, and until recently, parents were told that there was nothing that could be done to slow down its progression.

articles.title: HDC finds Southland Hospital failed pregnant woman whose baby was stillborn
articles.description: The woman says the doctor brushed off her concerns about her baby's slow growth rate.

articles.description: It all comes down to a major shift in how our nervous system commands the muscles

articles.description: New research identifies a muscle protein called NOX4 that declines with age and inactivity, potentially explaining why exercise keeps people stronger.

articles.description: Confirmed Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo have spiked up to 782.

articles.title: Worried about your child’s sleep? Keep screens out of bedrooms and limit iPads before bed
articles.description: Sleep can be one of the trickiest things for families. New research closely examines the relationship between sleep and screen-use in young children.

articles.description: Distrust in health care during infectious disease outbreaks can have devastating consequences. Here’s what we can do about it.

articles.title: GLP-1 therapy associated with more dizziness and fainting
articles.description: Northwestern Medicine scientists have identified a safety concern associated with GLP-1 drugs. Using health record data, the research team tracked more than 42,000 adults already taking at least two types of blood pressure medications.

articles.title: Experts reveal one simple trick that could help fight cravings
articles.description: The simple hack that is tricking people into better eating habits

articles.title: Physical activity declines after starting GLP-1 obesity treatments
articles.description: Adults with obesity losing weight with glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist medications significantly decreased their physical activity, which is essential to protect muscle, according to a study being presented Saturday at ENDO 2026, the Endocrine Society's annual meeting in Chicago, Ill.

articles.title: New Ontario task force to target dementia care gaps
articles.description: A new Ontario Dementia Task Force, involving more than 35 organizations, will spend the next year creating a care coordination model.

articles.title: AI model could improve screening for primary aldosteronism
articles.description: A study of a new AI model examining 30 years of routine electronic health records (EHR) data could improve screening for primary aldosteronism, a leading cause of high blood pressure that is often unrecognized but increases patients' risk of cardiovascular complications, according to a study being presented Saturday at ENDO 2026, the Endocrine Society's annual meeting in Chicago, Ill.

articles.title: Research offering more insight into how to reduce the risk of dementia
articles.description: January is Alzheimer’s Awareness Month and in the Cochrane and Temiskaming districts alone, there are more than 2,400 people living with dementia.

articles.title: U.S. losing Reaper drones in the Mideast without any clear plan to replace them
articles.description: The U.S. Air Force has lost nearly 1/3 of the available fleet of critical Reaper drones in Middle East conflicts since the start of the Trump administration, including more than two dozen in the Iran war.

articles.title: New guideline recommends tailored care for precocious puberty
articles.description: Some subgroups of children with precocious puberty-such as older girls with slowly progressing puberty-may not need the same level of testing or treatment, according to a new Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline released today.

articles.title: How many cups of coffee a day lower the risk of dementia?
articles.description: A new study has revealed the link between coffee and brain health. So how much exactly should you drink? And why, despite the encouraging results – this is not yet the end of the story.

articles.title: This is the probiotic dads may need after a Father’s Day feast

articles.title: No whey! Shortage fears as people want more and more high-protein products
articles.description: Whey is being added to everything from breakfast cereals and Pop-Tarts to crisps, bagels, tortillas, and Starbucks beverages

articles.title: ‘A huge spectrum of people coming together’: how parkrun made it to its millionth event
articles.description: Founded in 2004, the free weekly 5km event has grown into a global fixture of weekend life, taking place in parks, fields, seafronts and even prisons The millionth parkrun took place on Saturday, acting as a celebration of the community cohesion and public health benefit that the charity has been aiming to achieve across the past two decades. Those in attendance at the event in Bushy Park in west London included former Olympic champion Dame Kelly Holmes, as well as thousands of locals and parkrun fanatics alike. Continue reading...

articles.title: Dear Abby: Proper behavior around service dogs is critical
articles.description: Never distract, talk to or pet the service dog. Do not make eye contact with it either. Distracting my dog could cause a serious problem for me.

articles.title: Cain gets cancer update in Emmerdale and it’s not what he wants to hear
articles.description: Cain heads back to the hospital for an update following his cancer operation, and learns quickly that he has another fight on his hands.

articles.title: Even if the children seem "as usual," something in them has probably changed
articles.description: The security situation in Israel affects the children. The most important thing to understand during this period: Children do not always know how to say "I am afraid" or "I am anxious."

articles.title: NHS staff battling wave of food supplement disinformation
articles.description: Exclusive: Cancer charity says dispelling falsehoods gleaned from social media is now routine task for clinicians Social media misinformation about the use of dietary supplements such as turmeric, St John’s wort and magnesium is now so common that dispelling online claims has become a routine part of NHS clinicians work. Two out of five frontline health workers say they encounter patients who raise inaccurate or misleading information about supplements at least once a week. Continue reading...

articles.title: No gym? No problem – here’s how to build strength anywhere
articles.description: In this week’s Well Enough newsletter, Harry Bullmore explores why you don’t need a gym to build strength, and how bodyweight training and simple resistance work can deliver real results anywhere, as long as you focus on consistency over complexity

articles.title: I tried a Chief Medical Officer's hack to fall asleep fast after waking up at 3 a.m. and it worked — here's why
articles.description: Sleep expert Dr. Carlos Nunez says this simple mental trick "works every time" when he can't sleep — so I put it to the test during an anxious 3 a.m. wake-up. Here is how it works and why it cured my middle insomnia.

articles.title: Cheryl’s Cookies founder, entrepreneur dies at 74
articles.description: The founder of a popular cookie business and an Ohio native died Saturday morning at age 74 after a battle with Parkinson’s disease
